_comment: HIPAA Compliance Pack for UiPath Automation Ops
_description: HIPAA Security Rule — UiPath governance settings mapped to 45 CFR Part 164 Technical, Physical, and Administrative Safeguards.
_usage: Version 1.0 — initial draft. Apply with: python3 uipath_governance.py --mode configure --pack hipaa_policies.json --no-dry-run --jwt '...' --cookie '...'
_format: Each field is a rich object: { type, default, description, value, clause_ref, rationale, requirement, mapping_ids }. The 'value' key is what gets applied. All other keys are documentation.
_runtime_standards_comment: Standards evaluated at agent runtime by UiPathGovernanceMiddleware. Set here at compile time — runtime reads this automatically. Add/remove standards here to change what all agents in this tenant/group are evaluated against.

_requirement_legend.mandatory: MUST be set — HIPAA Security Rule requires this control
_requirement_legend.conditional_mandatory: MUST be set when condition applies (e.g. PHI is processed)
_requirement_legend.recommended: SHOULD be set — HIPAA best practice / NIST SP 800-66 guidance
_requirement_legend.optional: MAY be set — no direct HIPAA requirement

HIPAA-AITrustLayer-v1._description: AI Trust Layer — audit, PHI filtering, provider access controls (45 CFR §164.312(b), §164.312(a)(1), §164.308(b)(1))

global-control-toggle.description: Master enable/disable for the AI Trust Layer. Must be true for all sub-controls to take effect.
global-control-toggle.rationale: §164.306(a) requires covered entities to protect ePHI from reasonably anticipated threats. The master switch is the top-level gate ensuring all AI Trust Layer policies are enforced.

agents.rationale: §164.308(a)(1) requires risk analysis before enabling AI capabilities that process ePHI. Disabled by default; enable only after HIPAA risk assessment and BAA verification.

agenthub.rationale: Agent Hub provides access to external AI agents. Disabled until risk assessment confirms no ePHI exposure and BAA is in place with any relevant sub-processors.

apps.rationale: Apps may expose ePHI via user-facing forms. Disabled until access control review is complete.

jarvis.rationale: Autopilot uses generative AI. Disabled until risk assessment and BAA verification complete.

uia.description: Enable UiPath Intelligence (UIA) — core AI inference engine.
uia.rationale: UIA is the foundational AI engine used for document understanding and ML inference. Kept enabled; individual capabilities (documentunderstanding, etc.) are controlled separately.

documentunderstanding.description: Enable Document Understanding (OCR, ML extraction of documents including medical records).
documentunderstanding.rationale: Document Understanding processes document content which frequently contains ePHI (medical records, lab results). Disabled until HIPAA risk assessment and vendor BAA confirmed.

communicationsmining.rationale: Communications Mining analyzes message content which may contain ePHI. Disabled until data flow review confirms PHI de-identification or BAA is in place.

uipath-ai-rdk.description: Enable UiPath AI RDK (custom model deployment).
uipath-ai-rdk.rationale: Custom models via AI RDK require security review before ePHI may be processed. Disabled by default.

healing-agent.rationale: Healing Agent autonomously modifies automation behavior. Disabled pending risk assessment in clinical environments.

wingman.rationale: Wingman provides AI suggestions during runtime. Disabled until evaluated for ePHI exposure.

uipath-testmanager.rationale: Test Manager may store test artifacts containing ePHI. Disabled pending review.

saveprompts.description: Capture all AI prompts and responses for audit purposes.
saveprompts.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.312(b) Audit controls; §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(D) Information system activity review
saveprompts.rationale: §164.312(b) requires mechanisms to record and examine ePHI activity. Logging all AI I/O provides the audit trail required for HIPAA compliance and breach investigation.

traces-ttl-enforcement.description: Enforce the traces-ttl retention window — discard logs older than the configured TTL.
traces-ttl-enforcement.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.312(b) Audit controls; §164.316(b)(2)(i) Documentation retention (6 years)
traces-ttl-enforcement.rationale: §164.316(b)(2)(i) requires retaining documentation for 6 years from creation or last effective date. TTL enforcement ensures UiPath trace logs are retained for the configured window. For 6-year HIPAA archival, supplement with an external SIEM pipeline.

traces-ttl.description: Duration for which AI trace logs are retained in the UiPath platform cache (e.g. '30d', '90d', '365d').
traces-ttl.rationale: HIPAA requires 6-year retention. Set to maximum platform window (365d) and route to external SIEM for long-term archival beyond the platform TTL.

traces-ttl-effective.description: Effective TTL actually applied after enforcement evaluation.
traces-ttl-effective.rationale: Must match traces-ttl to confirm enforcement is active.

traces-disable-insights.description: Disable AI Insights analytics (prevents ePHI from being included in aggregated telemetry).
traces-disable-insights.rationale: AI Insights aggregates usage telemetry that may include ePHI fragments. Disabled to prevent inadvertent ePHI disclosure through analytics pipelines not covered by BAA.

container.description: In-flight PII/PHI detection settings per capability.
container.rationale: §164.312(e)(2)(ii) requires encryption of ePHI in transit. In-flight PII scanning detects and blocks PHI before it reaches external AI models, satisfying both transmission security and de-identification requirements.

pii-entity-table.description: HIPAA 18 PHI identifier categories configured for PII scanning at 0.5 confidence threshold. Covers all 18 HIPAA identifiers (§164.514(b)(2)).
pii-entity-table.rationale: §164.514(b)(2) enumerates 18 PHI identifier types that must be removed for de-identification. All 18 categories enabled at 0.5 confidence threshold to detect and block PHI before transmission to AI models.

anthropic-control-toggle.description: Enable Anthropic Claude as an AI provider.
anthropic-control-toggle.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.308(b)(1) Business Associate Contracts; §164.314(a)(2) Business Associate Contract requirements
anthropic-control-toggle.rationale: §164.308(b)(1) requires a signed Business Associate Agreement (BAA) before transmitting ePHI to any third-party processor. Anthropic must have a BAA in place before enabling.

azure-openai-control-toggle.description: Enable Azure OpenAI as an AI provider.
azure-openai-control-toggle.rationale: Azure OpenAI requires a signed BAA (Microsoft offers a HIPAA BAA for Azure services). Disabled until legal confirms BAA covers this specific service and tenant configuration.

gemini-control-toggle.description: Enable Google Gemini as an AI provider.
gemini-control-toggle.rationale: Google Cloud offers a HIPAA BAA but it must be explicitly executed and the specific Gemini service must be listed. Disabled until BAA is confirmed.

HIPAA-Studio-v1._description: Studio — automation development controls (45 CFR §164.308(a)(1) Risk management, §164.312(b) Audit)

enforceAnalyzerBeforePublish.description: Require all analyzer rules to pass before a process can be published.
enforceAnalyzerBeforePublish.rationale: §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(B) requires implementing security measures to reduce risks to ePHI. Enforcing analyzer before publish prevents automations with known security/quality violations from reaching production.

enforceAnalyzerBeforeRun.description: Require analyzer rules to pass before local execution (development-time enforcement).
enforceAnalyzerBeforeRun.rationale: Prevents developers from running non-compliant automations even locally, reducing risk of ePHI exposure during development and testing.

enforceAnalyzerBeforePushCheckIn.description: Require analyzer rules to pass before source control check-in.
enforceAnalyzerBeforePushCheckIn.rationale: Gate at source control ensures all committed code is compliant, supporting auditability of the entire development lifecycle.

enforceCheckInBeforePublish.description: Require source control check-in before publishing — ensures every release is tracked in version control.
enforceCheckInBeforePublish.rationale: §164.312(b) requires audit mechanisms for ePHI activity. Version control provides traceability of all automation changes, supporting breach investigation and change management.

enforceReleaseNotes.description: Require release notes before publishing — documents the purpose and changes in each release.
enforceReleaseNotes.rationale: §164.316(b)(1) requires written policies and procedures. Release notes provide documented evidence of change rationale, supporting HIPAA audit requirements.

exportAnalyzerResults.description: Export analyzer results as part of the published package for auditing.
exportAnalyzerResults.rationale: Exported analyzer results provide an artifact proving code quality checks were run and passed, supporting HIPAA audit trail requirements.

analyzeRpaXamlFilesOnly.description: Restrict analyzer to RPA XAML files only (exclude test files).
analyzeRpaXamlFilesOnly.rationale: Set false to analyze all files including test files, maximizing coverage of ePHI risk detection across the entire project.

enableAiActivitySuggestions.description: Enable AI-generated activity suggestions in Studio.
enableAiActivitySuggestions.rationale: AI-generated code suggestions may introduce logic that inadvertently mishandles ePHI. Disabled to ensure developers retain full ownership and review of all automation logic in clinical contexts.

autoUpdatePackagesForAutopilot.description: Automatically update packages for Autopilot-generated projects.
autoUpdatePackagesForAutopilot.rationale: Auto-updating packages bypasses the validation process required for HIPAA compliance. All package updates must go through change management review.

includeSources.description: Include source files (.xaml) in published packages to support auditing and reproducibility.
includeSources.rationale: Including sources in published packages supports HIPAA audit requirements by allowing reviewers to inspect the exact code running in production.

enableAllAvailableActivities.description: Allow use of all available activity packages in Studio.
enableAllAvailableActivities.rationale: Restricting available activities limits the attack surface. Developers should only access activities approved for clinical use, reducing risk of inadvertent ePHI exposure via unapproved integrations.

allowPreviewPackagesInActivitiesPanel.description: Allow preview/pre-release packages in the activities panel.
allowPreviewPackagesInActivitiesPanel.rationale: Preview packages have not undergone full security validation. Prohibited in HIPAA environments where all components must be vetted and stable.

objectRepositoryEnforced.description: Enforce use of the Object Repository for UI descriptor management.
objectRepositoryEnforced.rationale: Centralized UI descriptors improve maintainability and reduce risk of brittle automations that mishandle ePHI due to UI changes.

separateRuntimeDependencies.description: Separate design-time and runtime dependencies for cleaner production deployments.
separateRuntimeDependencies.rationale: Separating dependencies ensures only tested, approved runtime packages are deployed, reducing the attack surface in production clinical environments.

allowedCompatibilityFrameworks.description: List of compatibility frameworks allowed for new projects.
allowedCompatibilityFrameworks.rationale: Windows-Legacy framework has reduced security support. Restricting to modern frameworks (Windows, Cross-platform) ensures current security patches and .NET security features are available.

HIPAA-Robot-v1._description: Robot — runtime execution controls (45 CFR §164.312(a)(1) Access control, §164.312(e)(1) Transmission security)

runtimeAnalyzerRules.description: Runtime analyzer rules enforced during execution. Each rule specifies a ruleId, action (Error/Warning/Verbose/Off), and parameters.

parameters.CustomMessage: HIPAA VIOLATION (45 CFR §164.312(a)(1)): This automation attempted to access an application or URL not approved for PHI handling. Execution halted. Contact your HIPAA Compliance Officer.

HIPAA VIOLATION (45 CFR §164.312(e)(1)): Outbound transmission of PHI requires encryption and recipient authorization. This communication has been blocked. Route through Action Center for compliance review.

runtimeAnalyzerRules.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.312(a)(1) Access control; §164.312(e)(1) Transmission security; §164.308(a)(1) Risk management
runtimeAnalyzerRules.rationale: RT-UIA-001: §164.312(a)(1) requires technical policies to allow only authorized persons to access ePHI. App/URL whitelisting enforces access control at runtime. RT-OUT-001: §164.312(e)(1) requires guard against unauthorized access to ePHI transmitted over electronic communications networks.

HIPAA-Assistant-v1._description: Assistant — workforce access controls and human oversight (45 CFR §164.308(a)(3), §164.312(a), §164.308(a)(6))

allowCustomWidgets.description: Allow installation of custom third-party widgets in the Assistant.
allowCustomWidgets.rationale: Custom widgets from untrusted sources may exfiltrate ePHI. Only approved UiPath widgets are permitted in clinical environments.

useOfficialFeeds.description: Restrict package feeds to official UiPath feeds only.
useOfficialFeeds.rationale: Official feeds ensure packages have been vetted by UiPath. Unapproved package sources could introduce components that mishandle ePHI.

widgets.description: Approved widget list. Only UiPath.Apps.Widget enabled; all marketplace/community widgets disabled.
widgets.rationale: Marketplace and community widgets are not vetted for HIPAA compliance. Only the official UiPath Apps widget is approved for clinical use.

allowRunOutsidePersonalWorkspace.description: Allow running automations outside the user's personal workspace.
allowRunOutsidePersonalWorkspace.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.308(a)(3)(ii)(A) Authorization and/or supervision; §164.312(a)(1) Access control
allowRunOutsidePersonalWorkspace.rationale: §164.308(a)(3)(ii)(A) requires that workforce members access ePHI only as appropriate for their role. Restricting execution to personal workspaces enforces the minimum necessary principle.

allowShareAutomationUrl.description: Allow sharing automation URLs with other users.
allowShareAutomationUrl.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.308(a)(3) Workforce security; §164.308(a)(4) Information access management
allowShareAutomationUrl.rationale: Sharing automation URLs could grant unauthorized access to automations that handle ePHI. Disabled to enforce access management controls.

allowLoggingLevelChange.description: Allow end-users to change the logging verbosity level.
allowLoggingLevelChange.rationale: §164.312(b) requires audit controls to record activity. Allowing users to reduce log verbosity could suppress the audit trail required for HIPAA compliance.

displayEDRProtectionStatus.description: Display endpoint detection and response (EDR) protection status in the Assistant.
displayEDRProtectionStatus.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(A) Risk analysis; §164.308(a)(6) Security incident procedures
displayEDRProtectionStatus.rationale: Displaying EDR status makes security posture visible to users and IT, supporting rapid identification of unprotected endpoints that may handle ePHI.

enableActionCenter.description: Enable Action Center for human-in-the-loop approval workflows.
enableActionCenter.rationale: HIPAA requires human oversight for high-risk actions involving ePHI (update_patient_record, prescribe_medication, send_referral). Action Center routes these decisions to authorized approvers.

enableStudioWeb.rationale: Studio Web expands the development surface area and attack vectors. Disabled in HIPAA environments where development must occur on managed, hardened workstations.

enableTaskCapture.description: Enable Task Capture (screen recording for process documentation).
enableTaskCapture.rationale: Task Capture records screen activity which may capture ePHI. Disabled to prevent inadvertent recording and storage of ePHI outside controlled audit systems.

enableTaskMining.description: Enable Task Mining (automated process discovery via screen/event capture).
enableTaskMining.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.312(a)(1) Access control; §164.514(b) De-identification; §164.308(b)(1) Business Associate Contracts
enableTaskMining.rationale: Task Mining captures detailed user activity data that may contain ePHI. Disabled; requires dedicated HIPAA assessment, data processing agreement, and BAA before use in clinical environments.

useLocalUserPreferences.description: Allow users to override governance settings with local preferences.
useLocalUserPreferences.rationale: §164.306(a) requires covered entities to ensure compliance of workforce. Local preference overrides undermine centralized governance and could allow individuals to circumvent HIPAA controls.

HIPAA-IntegrationService-v1._description: Integration Service — connector access controls (45 CFR §164.308(b)(1) Business Associate Contracts, §164.312(e) Transmission security)

defaultConnectorState.description: Default state for all Integration Service connectors. 'Disabled' means all connectors are off unless explicitly enabled.
defaultConnectorState.clause_ref: 45 CFR §164.308(b)(1) Business Associate Contracts; §164.312(e)(1) Transmission security; §164.308(a)(3) Workforce security
defaultConnectorState.rationale: §164.308(b)(1) requires a BAA with every Business Associate before ePHI may be transmitted. Disabling all connectors by default enforces a deny-all policy — connectors are only enabled after legal confirms BAA coverage for the specific third-party service.

mapped.title: AI Trust Layer Master Switch
mapped.notes: Top-level gate ensuring all AI Trust Layer HIPAA controls are active.

mapped.title: Risk Analysis — AI Feature Risk Assessment
mapped.notes: Formal risk analysis must be conducted before enabling any AI feature (agents, documentunderstanding, etc.). UiPath enforces the outcome (features off by default); the analysis itself is an organizational process.

mapped.title: Integration Service Default Deny
mapped.notes: All third-party connectors disabled by default. Enable only after BAA with each sub-processor is confirmed.

mapped.title: Third-Party AI Provider BAA Controls
mapped.notes: Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, and Google Gemini all disabled until signed BAA for the specific service is on file.

mapped.title: AI Prompt Logging (Audit Trail)
mapped.notes: Captures all AI I/O for HIPAA audit trail. Supplement with external SIEM for 6-year retention.

mapped.title: Audit Log Retention Enforcement
mapped.notes: Platform TTL max is 365d. Full 6-year HIPAA retention requires external archival pipeline (Orchestrator → Splunk/SIEM).

mapped.title: In-Flight PHI Detection & Blocking
mapped.value_to_set: pii-in-flight-* = true; all 19 PII categories enabled at 0.5
mapped.notes: Covers all 18 HIPAA PHI identifiers (§164.514(b)(2)). In-flight scanning prevents ePHI transmission to AI models.

mapped.title: AI Feature Access — Default Off
mapped.notes: All high-risk AI features disabled by default. Enable individually after per-feature risk assessment and BAA verification.

mapped.title: Document & Communications Processing — Default Off
mapped.notes: These capabilities directly process content likely to contain ePHI. Disabled until risk assessment and BAA complete.

mapped.title: Disable AI Telemetry/Insights
mapped.notes: Prevents ePHI fragments from entering aggregated analytics pipelines not covered by BAA.

mapped.title: Analyzer Enforcement (Publish/Run/Check-In)
mapped.notes: Ensures security and quality analysis gates are enforced throughout the development lifecycle.

mapped.title: Source Control & Release Documentation
mapped.notes: Version control + release notes provide the documented change record required by HIPAA.

mapped.title: AI-Generated Code Controls
mapped.notes: Developers must own and review all automation code. AI-generated suggestions disabled to maintain accountability.

mapped.title: Activity & Package Restrictions
mapped.notes: Restricts development to approved, vetted activity packages only.

mapped.title: Audit Artifacts in Published Packages
mapped.notes: Analyzer results and sources in packages support post-deployment audit and reproducibility.

mapped.title: Runtime App/URL Access Control (RT-UIA-001)
mapped.notes: Enforces application and URL whitelisting at runtime. Configure whitelistApps/URLs per approved clinical app inventory.

mapped.title: Runtime Outbound Transmission Control (RT-OUT-001)
mapped.notes: Blocks unauthorized outbound email/data transmission containing ePHI. Routes to Action Center for compliance review.

mapped.title: Widget & Package Feed Restrictions
mapped.notes: Only UiPath.Apps.Widget approved. Marketplace/community widgets disabled to prevent ePHI exfiltration via unvetted components.

mapped.title: Workforce Access Restrictions — Run & Share
mapped.notes: Enforces minimum necessary access principle. Users can only run automations in their own workspace.

mapped.title: Human-in-the-Loop — Action Center
mapped.notes: Routes high-risk clinical actions (update_patient_record, prescribe_medication, send_referral) to authorized human approvers.

mapped.title: Audit Integrity — Logging & EDR Visibility
mapped.notes: Prevents users from suppressing audit logs; displays endpoint security status for operational awareness.

mapped.title: Studio Web Disabled
mapped.notes: Development must occur on managed, hardened workstations. Browser-based development expands attack surface.

mapped.title: Object Repository & Dependency Management
mapped.notes: Centralized UI descriptors and clean runtime dependencies reduce brittleness and attack surface in clinical automations.

mapped.title: Modern Framework Enforcement
mapped.notes: Windows-Legacy framework excluded to ensure modern .NET security features and current patch levels.

mapped.title: Autonomous Agent Features — Default Off
mapped.notes: Autonomous features that modify or observe automation behavior disabled pending HIPAA risk assessment.

mapped.title: Screen Capture Capabilities Disabled
mapped.notes: Screen recording and event capture could inadvertently capture ePHI. Disabled; Task Mining requires dedicated HIPAA assessment and BAA before any clinical use.

mapped.title: Governance Override Prevention
mapped.notes: Prevents workforce members from overriding centralized HIPAA governance settings via local preferences.

not_mapped.title: Formal HIPAA Risk Analysis
not_mapped.gap_description: UiPath enforces outcomes of risk analysis (features off by default) but cannot perform the formal §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(A) risk analysis itself. A documented organizational risk assessment is required.
not_mapped.compensating_control: Conduct formal HIPAA risk analysis per NIST SP 800-66r2. Document threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and impact for all systems handling ePHI.

not_mapped.title: HIPAA Security Officer Designation
not_mapped.gap_description: No UiPath control exists for designating a HIPAA Security Officer. This is an organizational requirement.
not_mapped.compensating_control: Formally designate a HIPAA Security Officer. Document the appointment and responsibilities. Ensure this individual reviews UiPath governance settings.

not_mapped.title: Workforce HIPAA Security Training
not_mapped.gap_description: UiPath has no built-in training module. Workforce must be trained on HIPAA security policies.
not_mapped.compensating_control: Implement annual HIPAA security awareness training for all staff accessing ePHI via UiPath automations. Track completion.

not_mapped.title: Security Incident Response Plan
not_mapped.gap_description: UiPath surfaces audit logs and EDR status but does not provide an incident response workflow.
not_mapped.future_uipath_control: Action Center could be used for incident routing
not_mapped.compensating_control: Maintain a documented security incident response plan. Integrate UiPath audit logs with SIEM for automated alerting. Route incidents through Action Center if using UiPath.

not_mapped.title: Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery
not_mapped.gap_description: No UiPath governance field controls backup, recovery, or contingency procedures for ePHI.
not_mapped.compensating_control: Maintain tested data backup and disaster recovery plans for UiPath Orchestrator and audit log storage. Include UiPath in BCP/DR exercises.

not_mapped.title: Physical Access Controls for UiPath Infrastructure
not_mapped.gap_description: UiPath governance policies control software behavior only. Physical access to servers, workstations, and data centers running UiPath is outside policy scope.
not_mapped.future_uipath_control: None — physical controls are infrastructure layer
not_mapped.compensating_control: Implement physical access controls per §164.310: facility access controls, workstation use policies, device/media disposal procedures. For cloud deployments, verify CSP physical compliance.

not_mapped.title: Unique User ID per Workforce Member
not_mapped.gap_description: UiPath relies on external IdP (Active Directory, Azure AD, Okta) for unique user identification. No UiPath governance field enforces this.
not_mapped.future_uipath_control: None — identity is managed by IdP
not_mapped.compensating_control: Configure UiPath to use federated authentication. Enforce unique user accounts in the IdP. Prohibit shared credentials. Audit user accounts quarterly.

not_mapped.title: Automatic Session Timeout
not_mapped.gap_description: Session timeout for the UiPath web console is not configurable via governance policy fields. Controlled at the platform/IdP level.
not_mapped.compensating_control: Configure automatic logoff in the UiPath cloud portal settings and/or IdP. Set session timeout to 15 minutes of inactivity per NIST SP 800-66 guidance.

not_mapped.title: ePHI Encryption at Rest
not_mapped.gap_description: UiPath governance policies control data flow and access; encryption of stored ePHI (in Orchestrator DB, audit logs) is not configurable via policy fields.
not_mapped.future_uipath_control: None — encryption at rest is infrastructure layer
not_mapped.compensating_control: Ensure all UiPath data stores (Orchestrator DB, storage buckets, audit log archives) use AES-256 encryption at rest. For cloud: verify CSP encryption settings. For on-premise: deploy encrypted volumes.

not_mapped.title: Long-Term ePHI Archival (6-Year Retention)
not_mapped.gap_description: UiPath traces-ttl has a maximum of 365 days. HIPAA §164.316(b)(2)(i) requires 6-year documentation retention. Platform TTL alone is insufficient.
not_mapped.future_uipath_control: Extended retention or SIEM export configuration (roadmap)
not_mapped.compensating_control: Deploy an audit log pipeline: UiPath Orchestrator → SIEM (Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel, etc.) with 6-year retention policy. Ensure SIEM vendor has signed HIPAA BAA.
